description: 100 pronunciation drills for the Speaking Lab. One is chosen at random each time a user opens the lab. Mix of greetings, numbers, everyday phrases, tongue twisters, tricky phonemes (TH/R/L/V/W), and character-driven lines.

exercises.target: Good morning. How are you today?
exercises.pt_hint: Bom dia. Como você está hoje?

exercises.target: Hello! My name is Bia and I am from Brazil.
exercises.pt_hint: Olá! Meu nome é Bia e eu sou do Brasil.

exercises.target: Nice to meet you. See you tomorrow.
exercises.pt_hint: Prazer em conhecer. Até amanhã.

exercises.target: One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten.
exercises.pt_hint: Um, dois, três... até dez.

exercises.target: Thirteen, thirty, fourteen, forty, fifteen, fifty.
exercises.pt_hint: Cuidado: -teen vs -ty.

exercises.target: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday.
exercises.pt_hint: Dias da semana — atenção em Wednesday.

exercises.target: This is my mother. This is my brother.
exercises.pt_hint: Esta é minha mãe. Este é meu irmão.

exercises.target: I would like a hamburger and a glass of water, please.
exercises.pt_hint: Eu gostaria de um hambúrguer e um copo de água, por favor.

exercises.target: Excuse me, where is the bus station?
exercises.pt_hint: Com licença, onde é a rodoviária?

exercises.target: I am happy because today is a good day.
exercises.pt_hint: Estou feliz porque hoje é um bom dia.

exercises.target: Think, thank, thirty, thirsty, Thursday.
exercises.pt_hint: TH surdo: língua entre os dentes, sem voz.

exercises.target: This, that, these, those, there, they.
exercises.pt_hint: TH sonoro: língua entre os dentes, com voz.

exercises.target: I think three things are thirsty on Thursday.
exercises.pt_hint: Aquecimento do TH surdo repetido.

exercises.target: Rafa runs a red food truck on the road.
exercises.pt_hint: R americano: língua pra trás, sem vibrar como em português.

exercises.target: Very well, we want a warm wave.
exercises.pt_hint: V morde o lábio. W é só boca arredondada.

exercises.target: Yesterday I walked to the beach and watched the sunset.
exercises.pt_hint: Verbos regulares no passado: -ed pronunciado /t/.

exercises.target: She wanted to dance but she decided to rest.
exercises.pt_hint: Verbos terminados em T/D pedem /ɪd/: wanted, decided.

exercises.target: My brother is taller than me, but I am faster than him.
exercises.pt_hint: Comparativos: -er + than.

exercises.target: I usually wake up at seven and drink a cup of coffee.
exercises.pt_hint: Rotina — present simple com advérbios de frequência.

exercises.target: Right now, Cosmo is sleeping under the table.
exercises.pt_hint: Ação em andamento: be + -ing.

exercises.target: Please pick up the phone and call me back.
exercises.pt_hint: Pick up = atender. Call back = ligar de volta.

exercises.target: Where did you go last weekend, and who did you see?
exercises.pt_hint: Perguntas no passado com DID.

exercises.target: It was raining all day, but now the sun is shining.
exercises.pt_hint: Tempo — past continuous contrastado com present continuous.

exercises.target: Could I have the bill, please? And a glass of water.
exercises.pt_hint: Pedindo a conta no restaurante.

exercises.target: The flight to London leaves at half past six in the morning.
exercises.pt_hint: Horário com half past.

exercises.target: Thirty-three thirsty thunderbirds thought through Thursday.
exercises.pt_hint: Trava-língua clássico de TH.

exercises.target: Really rare red roses rarely rot.
exercises.pt_hint: R em início de palavra.

exercises.target: She watched, she listened, she laughed, she decided.
exercises.pt_hint: /t/, /d/, /t/, /ɪd/ — quatro endings em ordem.

exercises.target: Turn it off. Pick it up. Put it on.
exercises.pt_hint: Separação obrigatória com pronome no phrasal verb.

exercises.target: I've been waiting. They're coming. We shouldn't have left.
exercises.pt_hint: Contrações — foco em fluência.

exercises.target: I have lived in Praia do Sol for fifteen years.
exercises.pt_hint: Present perfect com FOR — ação que continua no presente.

exercises.target: Honestly, I think we should reconsider the entire plan.
exercises.pt_hint: Dando opinião com HONESTLY + SHOULD.

exercises.target: If I had a million dollars, I would travel around the world.
exercises.pt_hint: Second conditional — hipotético presente/futuro.

exercises.target: She told me that she had already finished the report.
exercises.pt_hint: Reported speech com past perfect.

exercises.target: I cannot put up with this noise any longer.
exercises.pt_hint: Put up with = tolerar.

exercises.target: I am afraid I do not quite agree with that approach.
exercises.pt_hint: Discordância diplomática em reunião.

exercises.target: When I arrived, the plane had already left the runway.
exercises.pt_hint: Past perfect + past simple.

exercises.target: The man who stole my bike yesterday was wearing a blue hoodie.
exercises.pt_hint: Oração adjetiva restritiva.

exercises.target: She sells seashells by the seashore.
exercises.pt_hint: Clássico da pronúncia do S/SH.

exercises.target: Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers.
exercises.pt_hint: Explosão do P — controle do sopro.

exercises.target: The new library is being built on the other side of the river.
exercises.pt_hint: Passive continuous presente.

exercises.target: If I had studied harder, I would be fluent in English now.
exercises.pt_hint: Mixed conditional — passado -> presente.

exercises.target: Not only did she finish early, but she also got the highest score.
exercises.pt_hint: Inversão dramática com NOT ONLY.

exercises.target: Ladies and gentlemen, thank you for joining us this evening.
exercises.pt_hint: Abertura formal de evento.

exercises.target: At the end of the day, it is the thought that counts.
exercises.pt_hint: Idiom de fechamento.

exercises.target: My name is Bia and I love surfing at Praia do Sol.
exercises.pt_hint: Frase da Bia — surfista de 16 anos.

exercises.target: Welcome to Tropical Bites. What can I get you?
exercises.pt_hint: Frase do Rafa — food truck.

exercises.target: Practice is the key. Listen, read aloud, and write your own examples.
exercises.pt_hint: Frase do Mr. Kumar — filosofia da escola.

exercises.target: Cosmo is a good dog. Cosmo likes the beach and a tasty bone.
exercises.pt_hint: Frase sobre o Cosmo.

exercises.target: Thank you for listening. Every small step adds up.
exercises.pt_hint: Fechamento motivacional.

exercises.target: I would like to schedule a meeting for next Tuesday at three.
exercises.pt_hint: Agendar reunião para terça às três.

exercises.target: Please send me the report by the end of the day.
exercises.pt_hint: Mandar o relatório até o fim do dia.

exercises.target: Our team has exceeded the quarterly targets by twenty percent.
exercises.pt_hint: Excedemos as metas trimestrais em vinte por cento.

exercises.target: I am afraid the budget does not allow for that this quarter.
exercises.pt_hint: Infelizmente o orçamento não permite isso neste trimestre.

exercises.target: Let us circle back to this topic after lunch.
exercises.pt_hint: Vamos retomar esse tópico depois do almoço.

exercises.target: Could you walk me through the main findings of the study?
exercises.pt_hint: Pode me explicar as principais conclusões do estudo?

exercises.target: We need to align on the deliverables before the client call.
exercises.pt_hint: Alinhar os entregáveis antes da call com o cliente.

exercises.target: The proposal outlines three scenarios with distinct risk profiles.
exercises.pt_hint: A proposta descreve três cenários com perfis de risco distintos.

exercises.target: I would appreciate your feedback on the draft by Thursday.
exercises.pt_hint: Agradeceria seu feedback sobre o rascunho até quinta.

exercises.target: Thank you for bearing with us during the transition period.
exercises.pt_hint: Obrigado pela paciência durante o período de transição.

exercises.target: That aligns perfectly with our strategic priorities for the year.
exercises.pt_hint: Isso se alinha com nossas prioridades do ano.

exercises.target: We should escalate this to management before it gets out of hand.
exercises.pt_hint: Devemos escalar isso antes que fuja do controle.

exercises.target: The invoice is overdue — could you process the payment today?
exercises.pt_hint: A fatura está atrasada — pode processar hoje?

exercises.target: My direct report called in sick this morning.
exercises.pt_hint: Meu subordinado direto avisou que está doente hoje.

exercises.target: I want to follow up on the email I sent last Friday.
exercises.pt_hint: Dar follow-up no email que mandei na sexta.

exercises.target: Could you pass me the salt, please?
exercises.pt_hint: Pode me passar o sal?

exercises.target: I need to pick up my laundry before six.
exercises.pt_hint: Preciso buscar a roupa na lavanderia antes das seis.

exercises.target: The washing machine is broken again — we need a new one.
exercises.pt_hint: A máquina de lavar quebrou de novo.

exercises.target: I forgot my wallet at home, could you pay for now?
exercises.pt_hint: Esqueci a carteira em casa, pode pagar por enquanto?

exercises.target: The bus was late, so I missed my first class.
exercises.pt_hint: O ônibus atrasou, perdi a primeira aula.

exercises.target: My apartment's rent went up again this year — it is unbelievable.
exercises.pt_hint: O aluguel do meu apê subiu de novo esse ano.

exercises.target: I have been meaning to call the landlord about the leaky faucet.
exercises.pt_hint: Tô querendo ligar pro locador sobre a torneira vazando.

exercises.target: The traffic was a nightmare this morning because of the rain.
exercises.pt_hint: O trânsito tava um pesadelo hoje de manhã por causa da chuva.

exercises.target: I go to the gym three times a week, usually in the evening.
exercises.pt_hint: Vou à academia três vezes por semana.

exercises.target: I spilled coffee all over my new shirt — what a disaster.
exercises.pt_hint: Derramei café na camisa nova — que desastre.

exercises.target: Don't forget to lock the door when you leave.
exercises.pt_hint: Não esqueça de trancar a porta ao sair.

exercises.target: We ran out of milk, so I grabbed some on my way home.
exercises.pt_hint: Acabou o leite, passei pra comprar voltando.

exercises.target: It was great to see you again — let's hang out soon.
exercises.pt_hint: Bom te ver de novo, vamos sair em breve.

exercises.target: Happy birthday! I hope you have an amazing year ahead.
exercises.pt_hint: Feliz aniversário! Espero um ano incrível pra você.

exercises.target: I really appreciate you being there for me yesterday.
exercises.pt_hint: Agradeço muito ter me apoiado ontem.

exercises.target: We should catch up over dinner — my treat.
exercises.pt_hint: Vamos colocar o papo em dia num jantar — eu pago.

exercises.target: I know it is tough right now, but you are not alone in this.
exercises.pt_hint: Sei que tá difícil agora, mas você não está sozinho nisso.

exercises.target: I can't believe we have been friends for over ten years.
exercises.pt_hint: Não acredito que a gente é amigo há mais de dez anos.

exercises.target: Let me know if you need a ride to the airport tomorrow.
exercises.pt_hint: Me avisa se quiser carona pro aeroporto amanhã.

exercises.target: Thanks for having me over — dinner was delicious.
exercises.pt_hint: Obrigado pelo convite — o jantar estava uma delícia.

exercises.target: Are you free on Saturday? There is a concert in the park.
exercises.pt_hint: Tá livre sábado? Tem um show no parque.

exercises.target: I owe you an apology — I should not have said that yesterday.
exercises.pt_hint: Te devo desculpas, eu não deveria ter dito aquilo ontem.

exercises.target: My phone battery is at three percent — I need to find a charger.
exercises.pt_hint: A bateria está em três por cento — preciso de carregador.

exercises.target: The internet has been spotty all morning — I can barely join calls.
exercises.pt_hint: A internet tá oscilando a manhã toda — mal consigo entrar em chamadas.

exercises.target: Have you tried turning it off and on again?
exercises.pt_hint: Já tentou desligar e ligar de novo?

exercises.target: The software update broke half of the features I actually use.
exercises.pt_hint: A atualização quebrou metade das features que eu uso.

exercises.target: Could you share your screen so we can walk through it together?
exercises.pt_hint: Pode compartilhar a tela pra gente revisar junto?

exercises.target: I have a headache — do you have any painkillers?
exercises.pt_hint: Tô com dor de cabeça — tem analgésico?

exercises.target: The doctor recommended I drink more water and get more sleep.
exercises.pt_hint: O médico recomendou beber mais água e dormir mais.

exercises.target: I think I caught a cold — my throat is killing me.
exercises.pt_hint: Acho que peguei um resfriado — minha garganta tá me matando.

exercises.target: I need to book an appointment with the dentist next week.
exercises.pt_hint: Marcar consulta com o dentista na próxima semana.

exercises.target: I would like to confirm my flight to New York tomorrow.
exercises.pt_hint: Confirmar meu voo pra Nova York amanhã.

exercises.target: Is breakfast included in the nightly rate?
exercises.pt_hint: O café da manhã está incluído na diária?

exercises.target: Could I get a wake-up call at six thirty, please?
exercises.pt_hint: Pode me acordar às seis e meia por favor?

exercises.target: We got stranded at the airport because the flight was cancelled.
exercises.pt_hint: Ficamos presos no aeroporto porque o voo foi cancelado.
